title: ischaemum chordatum
intro: Ischaemum chordatum, also known as the rice grass or wild rice, is a type of grass native to Australia and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Ischaemum chordatum include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and improve digestion, as well as its potential to reduce the risk of certain cancers and neurological disorders. In herbal preparations, Ischaemum chordatum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used topically in poultices and salves to promote healing and reduce inflammation.
